title: The door you didn't know was a door
subtitle: A short visualisation for the moment an unexpected income route becomes real.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation practice from Day 32 of the MONEY program. The scene: a door in a familiar hallway that you notice for the first time, and what is behind it.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation for opening to unexpected income routes.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to build the felt sense of an unexpected income route opening.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from Day 32 of the MONEY program. A door in a hallway you have walked past a hundred times, which you have never opened because you assumed it was a cupboard.
Sit quietly. Close your eyes. Take a slow breath in and let it go.
Picture a hallway. It can be your own home, or a hallway from somewhere familiar. You have walked down it many times.
Today you notice a door in the hallway that you haven't paid attention to before. It is unremarkable. You assumed it was a storage cupboard, or that it led to nothing interesting.

You try the handle. It opens. Behind it is something good: a resource, a space, an opportunity. The specific shape of it doesn't matter. What matters is that it was there the whole time, and you didn't know it because you stopped looking after you mapped the obvious doors.
Now picture yourself walking through it. What is on the other side? Let the picture form without forcing it. Stay with it for a minute.
Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation for opening to unexpected income routes.
